title: Interviewing Hillary at Harvard
summary: On March 3, former Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton visited Harvard University at the invitation of Professors Nicholas Burns (HKS), Robert Mnookin (HLS), and James Sebenius (HBS) in one of her first public engagements since last autumn.
body: Secretary Clinton Discusses Challenges in Asia, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe During Harvard Visit The latest from Belfer See more On March 3, former Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton visited Harvard University at the invitation of Professors Nicholas Burns (HKS), Robert Mnookin (HLS), and James Sebenius (HBS) in one of her first public engagements since last autumn. While on campus, she met with Harvard undergraduates during a morning event organized by Harvard’s Institute of Politics. The remainder of her visit was dedicated to interviews and a luncheon as part of Harvard’s American Secretaries of State Project. Project faculty chairs Burns, Mnookin, and Sebenius interviewed Secretary Clinton at Loeb House where they discussed the challenges the United States faces in Asia, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe. The American Secretaries of State Project is a joint initiative of the Future of Diplomacy Project at Harvard Kennedy School, the Program on Negotiation at Harvard Law School, and Harvard Business School. Professors Burns, Mnookin and Sebenius previously interviewed former Secretaries of State Henry Kissinger , George Shultz , James Baker , Madeleine Albright , Colin Powell and Condoleezza Rice about the most consequential negotiations they conducted while serving in the nation’s highest foreign policy office. They plan to interview Secretary John Kerry in the autumn of 2017 at Harvard. Currently, the professors are completing a book, with Sebenius as lead author, entitled Kissinger the Negotiator: Lessons from Dealmaking at the Highest Level (forthcoming, HarperCollins). In addition, they plan to publish a comprehensive book about the lessons learned from their interviews with the former Secretaries. They also will create teaching case studies for Harvard and will produce a series of documentary films focused on their interviews and research. For more information about the American Secretaries of State Project, please visit belfercenter.org/ american-secretaries-state Recommended citation "Interviewing Hillary at Harvard." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School (Summer 2017). result.main_content_en: On March 3, former Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton visited Harvard University for one of her first public engagements since the previous autumn. The visit, at the invitation of Professors Nicholas Burns, Robert Mnookin, and James Sebenius, included a meeting with undergraduates and was primarily part of the American Secretaries of State Project. During an interview at Loeb House, the faculty chairs discussed with Secretary Clinton the significant foreign policy challenges facing the United States in Asia, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe. The project is a joint initiative of Harvard Kennedy School's Future of Diplomacy Project, Harvard Law School's Program on Negotiation, and Harvard Business School. It involves interviewing former Secretaries of State about their most consequential negotiations, with plans for a book, teaching cases, and documentary films based on these discussions.
result.main_content_zh: 3月3日，美国前国务卿希拉里·克林顿应哈佛大学教授尼古拉斯·伯恩斯、罗伯特·穆金和詹姆斯·塞贝尼乌斯的邀请访问哈佛大学，这是她自去年秋季以来首次公开露面之一。访问期间，她与哈佛本科生会面，并主要参与了哈佛大学“美国国务卿项目”的访谈和午餐会。在洛布之家，项目主席伯恩斯、穆金和塞贝尼乌斯教授对克林顿国务卿进行了访谈，重点探讨了美国在亚洲、中东和东欧面临的主要外交政策挑战。该项目是哈佛大学肯尼迪学院未来外交项目、哈佛法学院谈判项目以及哈佛商学院的一项联合倡议，旨在采访历任前国务卿，了解其任内最重要的谈判经验。项目成果将包括出版书籍、制作教学案例和系列纪录片。
